facts.text: Figure skating gets its name from the 'figures' (precise patterns) skaters once had to trace on the ice during competition.
The Axel jump is the only jump in figure skating that takes off from a forward edge, making it the hardest to master.
Sonja Henie of Norway won three consecutive Olympic gold medals in ladies' singles (1928, 1932, 1936) and later became a Hollywood star.
Torvill and Dean received a perfect score of 6.0 from every judge for artistic impression in their legendary 'Boléro' free dance at the 1984 Sarajevo Olympics.
The quad toe loop was the first quadruple jump landed in competition, achieved by Kurt Browning of Canada in 1988.
The ISU Judging System (IJS), introduced in 2004, replaced the old 6.0 scoring system after the 2002 Olympic judging scandal.
Michelle Kwan won five World Championship titles and nine U.S. Championship titles but never won an Olympic gold medal.
Jackson Haines, an American, is considered the father of modern figure skating for introducing dance and music to the sport in the 1860s.
A Salchow jump takes off from a backward inside edge and is named after Swedish skater Ulrich Salchow, who first performed it in 1909.
Yuzuru Hanyu became the first man to win back-to-back Olympic gold medals in men's singles since Dick Button in 1948 and 1952.
The death spiral in pairs skating requires the man to pivot while swinging his partner in a near-horizontal circle with her head close to the ice.
Stars on Ice, originally called the Tour of Champions, has been a major professional figure skating tour since 1986.
Frank Carroll coached both Michelle Kwan and Evan Lysacek, guiding Lysacek to Olympic gold in 2010.
The Lutz jump is named after Austrian skater Alois Lutz, who first performed it in 1913.
Tara Lipinski became the youngest Olympic champion in ladies' figure skating at age 15 in 1998.
Pairs teams perform throw jumps where the man launches the woman into the air to complete multi-rotation jumps.
The Grand Prix of Figure Skating series consists of six international events plus a Final for top qualifiers.
In the old 6.0 system, judges gave two marks: one for technical merit and one for artistic impression, each out of 6.0.
The first known skating club, the Edinburgh Skating Club, was founded in Scotland around 1744.
Katarina Witt of East Germany won Olympic gold in 1984 and 1988, becoming the first woman to win consecutive Olympic titles since Sonja Henie.
Nathan Chen won the 2022 Olympic gold medal in Beijing with a record-breaking free skate featuring five quad jumps.
The flip jump takes off from a backward inside edge with a toe pick assist, distinguishing it from the Lutz which uses an outside edge.
Brian Orser, known as 'Mr. Triple Axel,' went on to coach both Yuzuru Hanyu and Kim Yuna to Olympic gold.
Ice Capades was one of the first major touring ice shows, running from 1940 to 1995.
The World Figure Skating Championships have been held annually since 1896, making it one of the oldest international sporting events.
Virtue and Moir of Canada became the youngest ice dance Olympic champions when they won gold at the 2010 Vancouver Games.
A toe loop is the easiest quad jump, taking off from the back outside edge with a toe pick assist from the other foot.
The 2002 Salt Lake City Olympics pairs skating scandal led to a complete overhaul of the judging system.
Johnny Weir was known for his flamboyant costumes, including a swan outfit inspired by 'Black Swan' aesthetics.
Evgeni Plushenko of Russia won Olympic gold in 2006 and silver in both 2002 and 2014.
Kristi Yamaguchi won the 1992 Olympic gold medal, becoming the first Asian American woman to win the title.
The loop jump takes off and lands on the same back outside edge with no toe pick assist.
Rafael Arutunian has coached multiple Olympic and World medalists, including Nathan Chen and Ashley Wagner.
Disney on Ice began in 1981 and features Disney characters performing in ice show spectacles around the world.
The first artificial ice rink, the Glaciarium, opened in London in 1876.
Scott Hamilton won the 1984 Olympic gold medal and went on to found Stars on Ice and become a beloved TV commentator.
In ice dance, lifts cannot extend above the man's head, unlike pairs skating where overhead lifts are common.
The Grade of Execution (GOE) in the ISU system ranges from -5 to +5 and is added to or subtracted from a jump's base value.
Midori Ito of Japan was the first woman to land a triple Axel in competition, doing so at the 1988 NHK Trophy.
The Biellmann spin, where the skater pulls one foot overhead while spinning, is named after Swiss skater Denise Biellmann.
Adam Rippon became the first openly gay American athlete to win a medal at a Winter Olympics, earning team bronze in 2018.
The movie 'I, Tonya' (2017) depicted the infamous attack on Nancy Kerrigan orchestrated by associates of Tonya Harding.
The European Figure Skating Championships are the oldest annual international figure skating competition, first held in 1891.
Tatiana Tarasova coached multiple Olympic champions including Alexei Yagudin and the pairs team of Gordeeva and Grinkov.
Tonya Harding was the first American woman to land a triple Axel in competition at the 1991 U.S. Championships.
Yuzuru Hanyu set the world record for highest free skate score with 223.20 points at the 2019 Autumn Classic.
In the ISU Judging System, the total score is the sum of the Technical Element Score (TES) and the Program Component Score (PCS).
Pair skating originated in the 1890s in Germany and Austria and became an Olympic event in 1908.
The Cutting Edge (1992) is a popular romantic comedy about a hockey player turned pairs figure skater.
Dick Button was the first skater to land a double Axel in competition (1948) and the first to land a triple loop (1952).
Nancy Kerrigan was attacked on January 6, 1994, at the U.S. Championships in Detroit, in one of the most infamous incidents in sports history.
The component scores in ISU judging evaluate skating skills, transitions, performance, composition, and interpretation of music.
Alina Zagitova of Russia won Olympic gold in 2018 at just 15 years old by loading all her jumps into the second half of her free skate.
Ice dance became an Olympic discipline in 1976 at the Innsbruck Games.
The quad Lutz is one of the hardest jumps in figure skating, first landed in competition by Brandon Mroz in 2011.
Figure skating was the first winter sport included in the Olympics, appearing at the 1908 London Summer Games.
Kim Yuna of South Korea, nicknamed 'Queen Yuna,' won the 2010 Olympic gold with a world-record score.
The camel spin features the free leg extended backward parallel to the ice, resembling an arabesque in ballet.
Blades of Glory (2007) is a comedy starring Will Ferrell and Jon Heder as rival skaters forced to compete as a pairs team.
Irina Rodnina of the Soviet Union won ten consecutive World Championship titles in pairs skating (1969–1978).
The sit spin requires the skater to spin in a seated position with the skating knee deeply bent and the free leg extended.
Compulsory figures, where skaters traced patterns on the ice, were removed from competition after the 1990 World Championships.
Nathan Chen was known as the 'Quad King' for incorporating up to five different quadruple jumps in his programs.
The Grand Prix Final features the top six qualifiers from the Grand Prix series and is held annually in December.
Peggy Fleming won the only U.S. gold medal at the 1968 Grenoble Olympics and helped popularize figure skating on American television.
The triple Axel requires three and a half rotations in the air, making it the most difficult triple jump.
Gordeeva and Grinkov were one of the most beloved pairs teams in history; Sergei Grinkov tragically died of a heart attack during practice in 1995.
The first Winter Olympics, held in Chamonix in 1924, included figure skating as one of the original events.
Music with lyrics was banned in competitive figure skating until the ISU allowed it starting in the 2014–2015 season.
Skaters' costumes must follow ISU guidelines: excessive decoration that falls on the ice can result in deductions.
Yuzuru Hanyu was the first skater to break 300 total points under the ISU Judging System.
The U.S. Figure Skating Championships is the national championship that determines the U.S. team for Worlds and the Olympics.
Elena Berezhnaya and Anton Sikharulidze of Russia shared Olympic pairs gold with Sale and Pelletier of Canada in 2002 after the judging scandal.
The layback spin, performed primarily by women, features the skater arching the back while spinning upright.
Brian Boitano won the 1988 Olympic gold in the 'Battle of the Brians' against Brian Orser.
Quad jumps were rare in the early 2000s but became standard in men's programs by the 2018 Olympics.
The first World Figure Skating Championships were held in St. Petersburg, Russia, in 1896.
Virtue and Moir won their second Olympic gold in 2018, becoming the most decorated ice dancers in Olympic history.
A step sequence is a required element featuring a pattern of turns, steps, and movements performed across the ice.
The ISU (International Skating Union) was founded in 1892 and is the oldest governing body of an international winter sport.
Carolina Kostner of Italy was the first Italian woman to win a World Championship title in figure skating, in 2012.
In pairs skating, side-by-side jumps require both partners to perform the same jump simultaneously.
The 'kiss and cry' area is where skaters and coaches wait for scores after each performance.
Torvill and Dean's 'Boléro' free dance at the 1984 Olympics is widely considered the greatest ice dance performance of all time.
Evan Lysacek won the 2010 Olympic gold without attempting a quadruple jump, sparking debate about the importance of quads.
Skate blades are only about 4 millimeters (3/16 inch) wide, and skaters balance on this thin edge during spins and jumps.
The scratch spin (upright spin) starts slow and accelerates as the skater pulls their arms and free leg closer to the body.
Mao Asada of Japan was the first woman to land three triple Axels in a single competition.
Skaters typically retire competitive careers in their mid-to-late twenties, though some have competed into their thirties.
The backward crossover is one of the most fundamental skating techniques, used to build speed going backward.
Alexei Yagudin won the 2002 Olympic gold with his iconic 'The Man in the Iron Mask' free skate that earned four 6.0s.
The World Team Trophy is a team event where the top six ISU member nations compete collectively for points.
A combination jump consists of two or more jumps performed in sequence where the landing edge of one jump becomes the takeoff for the next.
Sasha Cohen was known for her incredible spirals and flexibility, earning silver at the 2006 Olympics.
The toe pick at the front of a figure skating blade has jagged teeth used for jumping and stopping.
Ice dancers are not allowed to perform jumps with more than one rotation, separating ice dance from pairs skating.
Patrick Chan of Canada was a three-time World Champion known for his exceptional skating skills and edges.
The first recorded figure skating competition took place in Vienna, Austria, in 1882.
Under-rotation on a jump (landing before completing the full rotation) results in a significant point deduction.
Yuzuru Hanyu attempted the quad Axel (4.5 rotations) at the 2022 Beijing Olympics, though he did not fully land it.
Dorothy Hamill's signature 'wedge' haircut became a national craze after she won Olympic gold in 1976.
In the ISU system, jumps performed in the second half of a program receive a 10% bonus to their base value.
Javier Fernández of Spain won two World Championship titles and made figure skating hugely popular in Spain.
Skaters use rocker and counter turns, brackets, and twizzles as part of their step sequences and connecting moves.
The 1961 U.S. Figure Skating team was killed in a plane crash in Belgium while traveling to the World Championships.
Throw jumps in pairs skating can send the woman traveling up to 8 feet in the air and covering 15 feet of distance.
Yu-Na Kim's 'James Bond' short program at the 2009 World Championships earned her a then-record score.
The flying sit spin begins with the skater jumping into the air before landing in the sit spin position.
Shizuka Arakawa won Japan's first Olympic gold medal in figure skating in 2006 with a graceful free skate featuring an Ina Bauer.
The edges of a figure skating blade are hollow-ground, creating two distinct edges (inside and outside) that skaters use for turns and jumps.
Tai Babilonia and Randy Gardner were the first American pairs team to win the World Championship, in 1979.
The free skate (long program) is typically four minutes for men and pairs, and four minutes for women.
Surya Bonaly of France was famous for performing illegal backflips in competition, including at the 1998 Olympics.
An edge call occurs when a skater takes off from the wrong edge on a Lutz or flip, resulting in a deduction.
The Challenger Series is a tier of international competitions below the Grand Prix that helps skaters earn world ranking points.
A skater's blade has a slight curve (the rocker), which allows for smooth transitions between forward and backward skating.
Nathan Chen overcame a disastrous short program at the 2018 Olympics to deliver a stunning free skate with six quads.
Madame Butterfly, Carmen, and Scheherazade are among the most frequently used pieces of music in figure skating programs.
The combination spin must include at least two basic spin positions: upright, sit, or camel.
Riverdance by Bill Whelan was used by numerous skaters and became one of the most overused pieces of music in the sport.
The twist lift in pairs skating involves the man throwing the woman into the air where she rotates and is caught.
Alexei Urmanov won the 1994 Olympic gold medal, becoming the first Russian man to win the title.
Before the ISU system, judges could give a perfect 6.0 score, which happened rarely and was the ultimate achievement.
Backstage at competitions, skaters warm up off-ice with jump harnesses, stretching, and off-ice rotation exercises.
The short program must contain specific required elements: jumps, spins, and a step sequence, all within a time limit.
Gracie Gold won two U.S. national titles and helped the U.S. win a team bronze at the 2014 Olympics.
Figure skating costumes can cost anywhere from $2,000 to over $10,000 for top competitive skaters.
The spiral sequence features the skater gliding on one foot with the free leg raised above hip level.
Yuzuru Hanyu's 'SEIMEI' free skate program, set to music from the Japanese film Onmyōji, became one of the most iconic programs of all time.
The Four Continents Championship is open to skaters from non-European ISU member countries.
A fall in competition results in a one-point deduction from the total score.
Ice dance was originally modeled on ballroom dancing, with compulsory dances based on specific rhythms like the waltz and tango.
Maxim Trankov and Tatiana Volosozhar of Russia won Olympic pairs gold in both team and individual events in 2014.
Brian Joubert of France was the first skater to consistently perform a quad toe loop-triple toe loop combination.
The lutz is identified by its takeoff from the back outside edge with a toe pick assist from the opposite foot.
Skaters typically begin serious training around age 5 or 6, though some start as young as 3.
The 'Miracle on Ice' refers to the 1980 U.S. hockey team, but figure skating has had its own share of Olympic miracles.
In the early days of figure skating, only men competed. Women first appeared at the World Championships in 1906.
Michelle Kwan's 'Lyra Angelica' and 'Salome' free skates are considered among the greatest programs ever performed.
Isabelle and Paul Duchesnay, representing France, revolutionized ice dance with their avant-garde programs in the early 1990s.
A toe Axel is sometimes mistakenly used by young skaters, but it does not exist as an official jump.
The warmup period before each group of competitors typically lasts six minutes, during which all skaters in the group practice simultaneously.
Kurt Browning of Canada was the first man to land a quad jump in competition, a toe loop at the 1988 World Championships.
Oksana Baiul of Ukraine won the 1994 Olympic gold at age 16, edging out Nancy Kerrigan by a fraction of a point.
Xue Shen and Hongbo Zhao of China won the 2010 Olympic pairs gold after four Olympics and 18 years of competitive skating.
The Ina Bauer is a move where the skater bends one knee while the other leg trails behind on the opposite edge, creating a dramatic arched position.
Technical specialists, not judges, identify and call the elements performed during a program in the ISU system.
Rudy Galindo became the oldest U.S. men's champion in 40 years when he won the 1996 title at age 26.
The short program is approximately 2 minutes and 40 seconds for singles and pairs.
Skaters often use custom-molded boots that cost $800 to $1,500 and blades that cost $400 to $900.
Maia and Alex Shibutani, known as the 'ShibSibs,' were the first Asian American ice dance team to win an Olympic medal.
A twizzle is a traveling turn on one foot that is a required element in ice dance.
Stephane Lambiel of Switzerland was known for his unmatched spinning ability, winning two World titles.
The first artificial ice rink in North America was built in New York City in 1879.
Meryl Davis and Charlie White won Olympic ice dance gold in 2014, becoming the first Americans to win the event.
The quad Salchow was first landed in competition by Timothy Goebel in 1998.
Pre-rotation on jumps, where the skater begins rotating before leaving the ice, is a debated topic in modern figure skating.
Sarah Hughes won the 2002 Olympic gold despite being in fourth place after the short program.
The number of revolutions in a spin affects its level designation, with Level 4 being the maximum.
Gabriella Papadakis and Guillaume Cizeron of France set multiple world records in ice dance and won Olympic gold in 2022.
A choctaw is a turn from one foot to the other that changes both direction and edge simultaneously.
The first skating manual was written by Robert Jones in 1772, focusing on figure skating technique.
The backflip is an illegal element in competition but remains one of the most crowd-pleasing moves in exhibition skating.
Japanese figure skating fans are known for showering the ice with Winnie-the-Pooh plush toys for Yuzuru Hanyu.
Tim Brown first popularized the flying camel spin in the 1940s, and it remains a staple element today.
In the 2014 Sochi Olympics, team figure skating was introduced as a new event for the first time.
The basic positions in figure skating spins are upright, sit, camel, and layback.
Carlo Fassi coached Peggy Fleming, Dorothy Hamill, John Curry, and Robin Cousins—four Olympic champions.
Skaters can lose points for time violations if their program is too short or too long.
The hydroblading move involves the skater leaning deeply to one side with their body nearly parallel to the ice.
The Junior Grand Prix series helps develop young skaters and serves as a pipeline to senior competition.
Yuna Kim's 'Danse Macabre' free skate at the 2009 Grand Prix Final stunned audiences with its artistry and technical difficulty.
A Mohawk turn involves changing feet while maintaining the same direction and switching edges.
John Curry of Great Britain won the 1976 Olympic gold and was known for bringing a ballet-like artistry to men's skating.
Debi Thomas became the first African American to win the U.S. and World Championships in 1986.
The quad flip was first landed in competition by Shoma Uno of Japan at the 2016 Team Challenge Cup.
Skate guards are rubber or plastic covers placed over blades to protect them when walking off the ice.
The music for the short program was limited to 2 minutes and 40 seconds plus or minus 10 seconds.
Ekaterina Gordeeva continued skating professionally after the death of her partner and husband Sergei Grinkov.
The 'Battle of the Carmens' at the 1988 Olympics saw both Katarina Witt and Debi Thomas skate to music from Carmen.
A delayed rotation jump is one where the skater holds an open position briefly before pulling in to rotate.
The ISU introduced a minimum age of 17 for senior international competition starting in the 2024-2025 season.
Boyang Jin of China was the first skater to land a quad Lutz in competition, at the 2015 Cup of China.
Training for elite figure skating typically involves 4-6 hours on the ice daily plus off-ice conditioning.
Robin Cousins of Great Britain won Olympic gold in 1980 and was known for his powerful jumping ability.
The 'well-balanced program' rules dictate how many of each element type a skater can include.
Isabelle Brasseur and Lloyd Eisler of Canada were known for their daring throw triple Axel in pairs skating.
Skating dresses traditionally feature stretch fabrics like spandex and are decorated with rhinestones and sequins.
The first World Championship for ice dance was held in 1952 in Paris.
Quad revolution in women's skating was led by Russian skaters like Alexandra Trusova, who landed multiple quads in competition.
The Axel jump is named after Norwegian skater Axel Paulsen, who first performed it in 1882.
Exhibition galas are held after major competitions, featuring medal winners performing non-competitive programs.
Fumio Igarashi of Japan performed the first triple jump in competition, a triple Salchow, in 1955.
Skaters must be members of an ISU member federation to compete internationally.
The lutz and flip look similar in the air, but the key difference is the takeoff edge—outside for lutz, inside for flip.
Yuzuru Hanyu retired from competitive skating in 2022 but continued performing in ice shows.
The three turn is the most basic turn in figure skating, named for the '3' shape the blade traces on the ice.
The U.S. Figure Skating Memorial Fund was established after the 1961 plane crash that killed the entire U.S. team.
Miki Ando of Japan was the first woman to land a quad jump (a Salchow) in competition, in 2002.
The Pairs Free Skate typically lasts 4 minutes and 30 seconds and features throw jumps, lifts, and side-by-side elements.
Skaters often carry lucky charms, stuff animals, or personal rituals before performing.
The International Skating Union establishes rules, sets competition standards, and oversees officials for figure skating worldwide.
Torvill and Dean were not romantically involved, despite the passionate chemistry in their performances.
Plushenko attempted to compete in four consecutive Olympics (2002, 2006, 2010, 2014), earning medals in three of them.
The edge quality of a skater—how smoothly and deeply they curve on inside and outside edges—is a key judging component.
Timothy Goebel was the first skater to land three quad jumps in a single free skate at the 2000 Skate America.
Costumes must not be overly theatrical or give the impression of excessive nudity, per ISU regulations.
Ice resurfacing between groups of competitors is done by a Zamboni machine to provide a fresh surface.
The flip jump was first performed by Swiss skater Werner Rittberger in the early 20th century.
Kaetlyn Osmond of Canada won World Championship gold in 2018 and Olympic gold as part of the Canadian team event.
Skaters can receive Level 1 through Level 4 for spins and step sequences based on difficulty features performed.
The 'Pooh rain' became a signature moment at Yuzuru Hanyu's competitions as fans threw stuffed bears onto the ice.
In pairs, the man must perform lifts where the woman is held overhead with one or both hands.
Tonya Harding and Nancy Kerrigan both competed at the 1994 Lillehammer Olympics despite the attack scandal.
The first enclosed artificial ice rink was the Glacier in London, opening in 1876 and using a combination of ether and glycerine.
The Grand Prix series includes Skate America, Skate Canada, Cup of China, NHK Trophy, Internationaux de France, and Rostelecom Cup.
Liz Manley of Canada gave a legendary free skate at the 1988 Olympics, earning a standing ovation and the silver medal.
Spins must be centered (minimal travel across the ice) to receive higher Grades of Execution.
The Charlotte spiral, named after German skater Charlotte Oelschlagel, involves bending forward with the free leg extended overhead.
Nathan Chen won three consecutive World Championship titles (2018, 2019, 2021).
The concept of 'cheated' jumps, where rotation happens on the ice rather than in the air, is a constant point of controversy.
Many elite skaters are homeschooled or attend online school to accommodate their intense training schedules.
The quad loop was first landed in competition by Yuzuru Hanyu at the 2016 Autumn Classic International.
Alissa Czisny was a two-time U.S. champion known for her elegant spins and layback.
The death spiral can be performed on inside or outside edges, both forward and backward.
Figure skating boots are stiffer than hockey or recreational boots to provide ankle support for jumps and spins.
Wenjing Sui and Cong Han of China won the 2022 Olympic pairs gold, becoming the first Chinese pair to win since Shen and Zhao.
The compulsory dance section was removed from ice dance in 2010, replaced by a short dance (later renamed the rhythm dance).
A 'Tano' jump variation features one arm raised overhead during the jump, adding difficulty and earning higher GOE.
Scott Moir is the most decorated figure skater in Olympic history with five medals across four Olympics.
Landing a jump on two feet instead of one results in a significant deduction and lower Grade of Execution.
The Flying Dutchman spin was popularized by Dutch skaters and involves a flying entry into a sit spin.
Yuzuru Hanyu won his first Olympic gold at the 2014 Sochi Games at age 19.
Kimmie Meissner became the first American woman to land a triple Axel in international competition in 2005.
The rhythm dance in ice dance has a specific theme or rhythm that changes each season (e.g., Latin, hip-hop, 1970s).
Hanyu's popularity in Japan is so immense that his image has appeared on postage stamps and special edition merchandise.
The split jump is a non-scored transition element where the skater leaps into a full split position in the air.
Jayne Torvill was born on October 7, 1957, in Nottingham, England, and Christopher Dean on July 27, 1958.
Side-by-side spins in pairs require both partners to perform the same spin simultaneously in sync.
The first documented skating competition was held in Austria in 1882, featuring special figures and free skating.
Skaters use chalk and rosin on their hands and boots to improve grip during lifts and jumps.
A butterfly spin is a gravity-defying move where the skater's body goes nearly horizontal while the legs scissor through the air.
Michelle Kwan was born on July 7, 1980, in Torrance, California.
The I-spin, where the skater holds the free leg straight up beside the head, was popularized by Sasha Cohen.
The quad Axel (4.5 rotations) has a base value of 12.50 points, the highest of any single jump in the ISU system.
Fumie Suguri was a five-time Japanese national champion and an important figure in growing the sport in Japan.
Clean programs (those with no falls or major errors) typically outscore programs with more difficult jumps that include mistakes.
The Lutz is the only jump where the skater approaches on a long backward glide on the outside edge of the opposite foot.
Tessa Virtue was born on May 17, 1989, and Scott Moir on September 2, 1987.
Alexandra Trusova of Russia was the first woman to land a quad Lutz in competition.
The bonfire spin is a dramatic variation where the skater's body contorts while spinning low to the ice.
Skating choreographers like David Wilson and Lori Nichol are almost as famous as the skaters they work with.
The 2022 Beijing Olympics were marred by a doping scandal involving Russian skater Kamila Valieva.
A step sequence must travel across the length of the ice and incorporate turns, steps, and body movements.
Katarina Witt was born on December 3, 1965, in Staaken, East Germany.
The triple-triple combination (two triple jumps in a row) is one of the most valuable jump combinations for women.
Ice shows like Stars on Ice provide retired competitive skaters with lucrative performing careers.
The ISU implemented video replay review for technical elements to help ensure accurate jump identification.
Jeff Buttle of Canada won the 2008 World Championship title with an elegant and clean performance.
In competition, each skating element receives a base value, which is then modified by the GOE for quality of execution.
Tara Lipinski was born on June 10, 1982, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
Scott Hamilton was born on August 28, 1958, and was diagnosed with testicular cancer in 1997 but returned to skating.
The backward death spiral requires the woman to arch backward until her head nearly touches the ice while being rotated.
Skaters sometimes use 'stroking exercises' as warm-ups, which involve pushing across the ice to build power and speed.
Cantilevering is a moves where the skater leans dramatically out of vertical while gliding on one foot.
The 2014 Sochi Olympics team event featured men's singles, women's singles, pairs, and ice dance.
The ISU World Standings are used to determine seedings and entries for major international competitions.
Mirai Nagasu became the first American woman to land a triple Axel at the Olympics, at the 2018 PyeongChang Games.
A Detroiter jump is a half-revolution jump used primarily as a connecting move between elements.
The Willy (walley) jump takes off from a back inside edge without a toe pick, making it unique among edge jumps.
Blades are sharpened regularly, as dull blades reduce grip and control on the ice.
Patrick Chan won Olympic silver in 2014 and gold as part of Team Canada's team event in 2018.
Alina Zagitova performed all seven jumping passes in the bonus second half of her Olympic free skate.
The difference between a flip and Lutz is the takeoff edge (inside for flip, outside for Lutz), but they look identical in the air.
Yuzuru Hanyu's fans organize elaborate banner displays and synchronized cheering at competitions.
The Russian Figure Skating Federation has been the dominant force in women's skating since the mid-2010s.
In the 1994 Olympics, both Tonya Harding and Nancy Kerrigan were on the U.S. team despite the ongoing investigation.
The crossover is the most basic way to gain speed in figure skating, performed in both forward and backward directions.
Nathan Chen was born on May 5, 1999, in Salt Lake City, Utah.
Evgeni Plushenko attempted the quad-triple-triple combination in competition, one of the most difficult jump combinations ever attempted.
Midori Ito of Japan won silver at the 1992 Olympics and was one of the most powerful jumpers in women's skating history.
Choreographic sequences are non-scored elements in ice dance that allow for artistic expression.
The Zamboni was invented by Frank Zamboni in 1949 and revolutionized ice maintenance for skating and hockey.
Skaters can be penalized for costume malfunctions, including decorations falling on the ice during a program.
Yuzuru Hanyu was born on December 7, 1994, in Sendai, Japan.
The first time figure skating appeared at the Olympics was in 1908 in London, 16 years before the first Winter Olympics.
A flying entry into a spin adds difficulty and can increase the spin's level designation.
Elvis Stojko of Canada was one of the first men to consistently perform quad-combination jumps in the 1990s.
Ice dance lifts have specific duration limits and must be executed with specific positions to earn maximum levels.
In pairs skating, the twist lift requires the man to throw the woman into the air for rotations before catching her.
Skating blades are typically made of tempered carbon steel, though some use stainless steel for rust resistance.
Denise Biellmann of Switzerland was the 1981 World Champion and invented the Biellmann spin where she pulled her foot over her head.
The rhythm dance replaced the compulsory dance and original dance components in ice dance competition.
Skaters often practice jumps while wearing a harness attached to a fishing-rod-like device operated by their coach.
The European Championships are held annually in January and are limited to skaters from European ISU member nations.
Michael Weiss was a two-time U.S. champion known for performing backflips in exhibition programs.
The 'flutz' is a common error where a skater takes off on an inside edge instead of the required outside edge for a Lutz.
Michelle Kwan won her first World Championship title in 1996 at age 15.
The quad revolution accelerated dramatically between 2016 and 2022 as more skaters added multiple quads to their programs.
Off-ice training for skaters includes ballet, Pilates, resistance training, and plyometrics.
The US Figure Skating Hall of Fame includes legends like Dick Button, Peggy Fleming, and Michelle Kwan.
A broken boot or blade during competition allows a skater time to make repairs before continuing.
Yuna Kim won Olympic gold at the 2010 Vancouver Games and silver at the 2014 Sochi Games.
The total cost of competitive figure skating at the elite level can exceed $35,000 to $50,000 per year.
Pairs skating requires immense trust between partners, especially during throw jumps and overhead lifts.
The short program was introduced to competition in 1973 to add a structured technical segment.
Bradie Tennell won the 2018 U.S. Championship with a flawless free skate.
Skaters sharpen their blades to specific specifications, with the hollow and rocker customized to their preferences.
The quad revolution was partly driven by revisions in the ISU scoring system that increased base values for quad jumps.
Roller skating and inline skating are used as off-ice training methods by many figure skaters.
Plushenko's 'Sex Bomb' exhibition program became one of the most iconic and entertaining exhibition performances in skating history.
Satoko Miyahara of Japan was known for her elegant artistry and consistency at the international level.
The scratch spin is typically the first spin taught to beginning skaters and involves a fast upright rotation.
Ice dance has been trending toward more athletic and less traditional ballroom-style programs in recent years.
The 1998 Nagano Olympics saw Tara Lipinski defeat Michelle Kwan for the gold medal in a dramatic upset.
The term 'crossfoot spin' describes a spin where both feet are on the ice, crossed at the ankles.
Daisuke Takahashi of Japan was the first Asian man to win an Olympic medal in figure skating, with bronze in 2010.
The ISU has gradually increased the minimum age for senior competition to protect young skaters' physical and mental health.
Jason Brown is known for his exceptional artistry and performance quality despite not attempting quadruple jumps consistently.
Pairs skating lifts are divided into five groups based on the hold and entry style.
The 'Rippon' jump variation, named after Adam Rippon, features both arms raised overhead during the jump.
Marina Zoueva coached multiple Olympic champion ice dance teams, including Virtue/Moir and Davis/White.
Skaters typically perform two programs at major competitions: a short program and a free skate.
The Worlds are typically held in March, serving as the final major competition of the skating season.
The 'lip' error refers to a flip jump taken off from an outside edge instead of the required inside edge.
Johnny Weir was born on July 2, 1984, in Coatesville, Pennsylvania.
Torvill and Dean's 'Boléro' started with them on their knees because the rules required the performance to be under 4 minutes and 10 seconds.
Kamila Valieva was 15 when she became the first woman to land a quad jump at the Olympics during the 2022 team event.
The ISU Congress meets biennially to vote on rule changes, age limits, and other governance matters.
Kristi Yamaguchi was born on July 12, 1971, in Hayward, California.
Landing on the toe pick ('toeing down') instead of the blade's edge is a common mistake that can cause falls.
The U.S. National Championships are typically held in January each year.
The donut spin features the skater holding the free leg in a circle behind the body while spinning.
Shen Xue and Zhao Hongbo's gold medal at the 2010 Olympics was a fairy-tale ending to their 18-year partnership.
Carolina Kostner was born on February 8, 1987, in Bolzano, Italy.
The 2018 PyeongChang Olympics saw the introduction of numerous quad jumps in women's skating for the first time.
Skaters must qualify through their national championships to represent their country at Worlds and the Olympics.
A triple Axel has 3.5 rotations and is worth 8.00 base points in the ISU system.
The 'Kerrigan attack' on January 6, 1994, remains one of the most covered stories in sports history.
Skaters sometimes switch coaches mid-career to gain new technical skills or a fresh perspective.
The Grand Prix assignments are determined by the ISU based on world rankings and previous season results.
Adam Rippon was born on November 11, 1989, in Scranton, Pennsylvania.
Choreography in figure skating programs typically tells a story or captures the mood and emotion of the music.
Jumps performed in combination must land on the same foot and edge from which the next jump takes off.
The Flying Camel spin starts with a jump into the air before landing in a camel spin position.
Joannie Rochette of Canada earned a bronze medal at the 2010 Olympics just days after her mother passed away.
Blade rockers come in different radiuses—a smaller radius helps with spins, while a larger one aids in jumping and stroking.
The 2010 Vancouver Olympics ice dance final between Virtue/Moir and Davis/White was one of the most anticipated events.
Skaters use proprioceptive training and balance exercises to improve their control on the ice.
A 'two-foot landing' is a deduction where the skater puts down the free foot upon landing instead of maintaining one foot.
The World Junior Championships showcase the next generation of skating talent from around the globe.
The combination spin changes position (camel to sit to upright, for example) and must include at least two positions.
Brian Orser won silver at both the 1984 and 1988 Olympics and later became one of the sport's most successful coaches.
Mao Asada was born on September 25, 1990, in Nagoya, Japan.
The 'Axel Paulsen Memorial Competition' was historically held in Norway to honor the jump's inventor.
Skaters often have sport psychologists to help manage performance anxiety and competition pressure.
The ladies' free skate was extended from 4 minutes to 4 minutes and 10 seconds under rule adjustments.
In ice dance, the required twizzle sequence must include matched synchronized twizzles by both partners.
Yuzuru Hanyu's autobiography became a bestseller in Japan and helped expand skating's fanbase.
The 1988 'Battle of the Brians' between Brian Boitano and Brian Orser is considered one of the greatest Olympic rivalries.
Skating blades have a toe pick with several teeth that grip the ice during takeoffs for toe jumps.
Toller Cranston of Canada was a pioneer of artistic skating in the 1970s, pushing the boundaries of expression on ice.
The reverse Biellmann spin is an extremely rare variation where the skater pulls their foot over their head from the opposite direction.
Skate Canada International is one of the oldest events in the Grand Prix series, held annually since 1973.
The 'Tano Lutz,' with one arm raised overhead during the jump, was named after Brian Boitano who popularized it.
Ekaterina Gordeeva published the memoir 'My Sergei' about her skating career and relationship with Sergei Grinkov.
The first woman to attempt a quad Axel in competition was Japanese skater Mao Shimada.
Zakrajsek, Brown, and Orser are among the coaches known for emphasizing artistry alongside technical skills.
Skaters sometimes suffer from overuse injuries including stress fractures, tendinitis, and hip injuries from repetitive jumping.
The ice at competition venues is typically maintained at a temperature of about -3 to -5 degrees Celsius.
Rika Kihira of Japan was the first woman to land a triple Axel-triple toe loop combination in competition.
US Figure Skating introduced a mental health initiative to support the wellbeing of competitive skaters.
The short lift in ice dance must last no more than six seconds and feature a specific pattern.
Figure skating programs are choreographed months in advance, with skaters spending the off-season perfecting them.
Yuzuru Hanyu survived the 2011 earthquake and tsunami in Sendai, which destroyed his home rink.
The haircutter spin is a layback variation where the skater grabs the free foot blade behind their head.
Vincent Zhou of the US was the first skater to land a quad Lutz at the Olympics during the 2018 team event.
Ice dancing costumes tend to match between partners, creating a unified visual presentation.
The quad toe loop has a base value of 9.50 points in the ISU Judging System.
The 'Phantom of the Opera' is one of the most frequently chosen pieces of music for competitive programs.
Judges sit in a panel typically consisting of nine members for major ISU championships.
Anna Shcherbakova of Russia won the 2022 Olympic gold medal amid controversy surrounding her teammate Valieva's doping case.
The falling leaf is a beginner move where the skater performs a series of swizzles while moving backward and rocking side to side.
Many former competitive skaters transition to coaching, choreography, or commentary after retiring from competition.
The quad flip has a base value of 11.00 points, making it the second-most valuable quad jump after the Axel.
